per_question.question: What is a customer?
per_question.answer:
In the business glossary, Customer is defined only partially as “An individual or organization that...” and the definition is marked incomplete. The same glossary says Client is “A person or company that purchases goods or services,” and notes that Client may be used interchangeably with Customer in some contexts.

The glossary also distinguishes related terms: Account Holder is the primary owner of an account and is similar to Customer but in an account-specific context, while End User is the actual user of a product or service and may differ from the purchaser.

In the schema, customer-related information is stored in the CUSTOMERS table, which includes identifiers and attributes such as customer_id or CustomerID, first and last name fields, email, phone, created date fields, account/status fields, customer_type, and customer_region. The data dictionary notes that this documentation is incomplete and contains possible duplicate column names.

BUSINESS GLOSSARY - Enterprise System
Last Updated: 2024-03-15 Status: Partial (pending review) ========================================== CUSTOMER MANAGEMENT ========================================== Customer An individual or organization that... [definition incomplete] Client A person or company that purchases goods or services. (Note: may be used interchangeably with Customer in some contexts) Account Holder The person designated as the primary owner of an account. Similar to Customer but with account-specific context. End User The actual user of a product or service, may differ from the purchaser. ========================================== ORDER & TRANSACTION ========================================== Order A request to purchase products or services. Can be in various states. Transaction Similar to Order but more general. May refer to

Business concept 'Order Items' — The table structure matches an order line-item entity: order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, discount fields, and line_total are classic attributes of order details. The table comment explicitly names ORDER_ITEMS and notes it is also referenced as ORDER_DETAILS, making this mapping near-certain. is implemented by physical table ORDER_ITEMS (columns: order_item_id, order_id, product_id, ProductID, quantity, unit_price, UnitPrice, discount_percent, discount_amount, line_total, LineTotal).

Business concept 'Table naming: ORDER_ITEMS vs ORDER_PRODUCTS vs ORDER_DETAILS' is implemented by physical table PRODUCTS (columns: product_id, ProductID, product_name, ProductName, product_sku, sku, product_category, category, unit_price, current_price, cost_price, stock_quantity, quantity_in_stock, reorder_level, is_active, active_flag, created_at, updated_at, product_description).

Order Items: The table structure matches an order line-item entity: order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, discount fields, and line_total are classic attributes of order details. The table comment explicitly names ORDER_ITEMS and notes it is also referenced as ORDER_DETAILS, making this mapping near-certain.
Table naming: ORDER_ITEMS vs ORDER_PRODUCTS vs ORDER_DETAILS
per_question.expected_answer: The business glossary defines Customer as "an individual or organization that... [definition incomplete]". Related terms include Client (a person or company that purchases goods or services, sometimes used interchangeably with Customer), Account Holder (the primary owner of an account), and End User (the actual user, who may differ from the purchaser).
